questions.question_text: Which built-in Azure RBAC role grants full access to all Azure resources AND the ability to assign roles to other users?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Contributor grants full resource management but cannot assign roles or manage access.

b.explanation: Incorrect. Reader is a read-only role — no creation, modification, or access management.

c.explanation: Correct. Owner grants full access to all resources AND the ability to assign Azure RBAC roles to others.

d.text: User Access Administrator
d.explanation: Incorrect. User Access Administrator can manage access (assign roles) but cannot manage the resources themselves.

questions.question_text: A company needs to ensure all new resource groups in a subscription have an 'Environment' tag. Which Azure governance feature should they use?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Resource locks prevent deletion or modification but do not enforce tagging requirements.

b.explanation: Correct. Azure Policy can enforce tagging requirements using 'Require a tag on resource groups' or a custom Deny policy that blocks resource group creation without the required tag.

c.explanation: Incorrect. Management groups are containers for organising subscriptions — they do not directly enforce tags by themselves.

d.text: Azure Blueprints
d.explanation: Incorrect. Blueprints can include policies, but the core mechanism for enforcing tags is Azure Policy, not Blueprints.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum Microsoft Entra ID license required to use dynamic group membership rules?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Entra ID Free supports static group membership only — dynamic rules require P1 or higher.

b.explanation: Correct. Dynamic group membership requires Microsoft Entra ID P1 (or P2). P1 is the minimum tier that supports attribute-based dynamic membership rules.

c.explanation: Incorrect. P2 is not the minimum — P1 already includes dynamic group membership.

d.text: Microsoft 365 E3
d.explanation: Incorrect. M365 E3 includes Entra ID P1, but it is the P1 license that enables dynamic groups — not the M365 E3 designation itself.

questions.question_text: An administrator applies a ReadOnly lock to a storage account. Which of the following operations will STILL be permitted?

a.text: Upload a new blob to a container
a.explanation: Incorrect. ReadOnly prevents any write operations including uploading blobs.

b.explanation: Incorrect. ReadOnly prevents both modification and deletion.

c.text: List the access keys for the storage account
c.explanation: Incorrect. Listing access keys is treated as a write operation (POST) and is blocked by a ReadOnly lock.

d.text: View the storage account configuration and properties
d.explanation: Correct. ReadOnly allows read operations (GET requests). Viewing properties and configuration is a read operation and remains permitted.

questions.question_text: A user is assigned the Reader role at the subscription level. An administrator then assigns the Contributor role to the same user on a specific resource group. What is the user's effective access on that resource group?

a.text: Reader only — the subscription-level role takes precedence
a.explanation: Incorrect. RBAC role assignments are additive — there is no precedence based on scope level (unless a Deny assignment exists).

b.text: Contributor — the more specific scope assignment applies
b.explanation: Partially correct reasoning but wrong explanation. The user has BOTH Reader (inherited) AND Contributor on the resource group. The effective permission is the union, which is essentially Contributor.

c.text: Both Reader and Contributor — effective permissions are the union of all role assignments
c.explanation: Correct. RBAC permissions are additive. The user has Reader from subscription (inherited) plus Contributor from the resource group. The effective access is the union — essentially Contributor permissions on the resource group.

d.text: No access — conflicting roles cancel each other out
d.explanation: Incorrect. RBAC roles never cancel each other — they are always additive. Only Deny assignments can remove permissions.

questions.question_text: Which Azure Policy effect will PREVENT non-compliant resources from being created while logging the attempted violation?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Audit allows the resource creation but logs the non-compliance — it does not block anything.

b.explanation: Incorrect. Modify adds or changes properties on a resource during creation or update — it does not block resource creation.

c.explanation: Correct. Deny blocks the resource creation/update request if it violates the policy condition. The request is rejected with a 403 error.

d.text: DeployIfNotExists
d.explanation: Incorrect. DeployIfNotExists deploys a remediation resource if a required related resource does not exist — it does not block creation.

questions.question_text: What is the maximum number of levels a management group hierarchy can have below the Root management group?

a.explanation: Incorrect. 3 levels would be too few for large enterprise hierarchies.

b.explanation: Correct. Management groups support up to 6 levels of nesting below the Root management group (not counting the Root itself or subscription level).

c.explanation: Incorrect. The maximum is 6 levels below the Root management group.

d.text: Unlimited
d.explanation: Incorrect. Management group hierarchy is limited to 6 nesting levels below Root.

questions.question_text: An Entra ID user must reset their own password from outside the corporate network. Which feature enables this without requiring IT helpdesk involvement?

a.explanation: Incorrect. PIM provides just-in-time privileged role activation — it is not used for password resets.

b.explanation: Incorrect. Conditional Access controls access based on conditions — it does not provide self-service password reset functionality.

c.explanation: Correct. SSPR allows users to reset their own passwords using registered authentication methods (email, phone, authenticator app) without IT helpdesk involvement. Requires Entra ID P1 for on-premises writeback.

d.text: Identity Protection
d.explanation: Incorrect. Identity Protection detects risky sign-ins and users — it does not provide a self-service password reset portal.

questions.question_text: A company is consolidating Azure subscriptions from two acquisitions. They want to apply the same Azure Policies to all subscriptions with a single assignment. What should they use?

a.text: Assign policies to each subscription individually
a.explanation: Incorrect. While possible, this approach does not scale and must be repeated for every new subscription added.

b.text: Place all subscriptions under a management group and assign the policy to the management group
b.explanation: Correct. Management groups allow policy assignments that automatically apply to all subscriptions (and resource groups and resources) within the hierarchy. This is the recommended approach for multi-subscription governance.

c.text: Use resource groups to organise subscriptions
c.explanation: Incorrect. Resource groups are within subscriptions, not above them — they cannot span subscriptions.

d.text: Use Azure Blueprints to deploy policies to individual subscriptions
d.explanation: Incorrect. Blueprints deploy resources to subscriptions, but management groups are the right tool for inheritable policy scope across many subscriptions.

questions.question_text: A resource group is tagged with 'CostCenter=Marketing'. A new storage account is created in that resource group without any tags. Which of the following is true?

a.text: The storage account automatically inherits the 'CostCenter=Marketing' tag
a.explanation: Incorrect. Tags on a resource group do NOT automatically inherit to resources within it in Azure. Tag inheritance requires an Azure Policy with the 'Inherit a tag from the resource group' effect.

b.text: The storage account has no tags unless explicitly tagged or a policy enforces inheritance
b.explanation: Correct. Azure tags do not automatically inherit from resource groups to resources. An Azure Policy with the 'Inherit a tag from resource group' effect or the 'Append' effect must be used to propagate tags automatically.

c.text: Tags cannot be applied to resource groups, only to individual resources
c.explanation: Incorrect. Tags can be applied to resource groups, subscriptions, and individual resources.

d.text: The storage account inherits all tags but they can be overridden
d.explanation: Incorrect. Inheritance does not happen automatically — tags must be explicitly set on each resource or enforced via policy.

questions.question_text: Which hybrid identity authentication method synchronises a hash of the password hash from on-premises Active Directory to Microsoft Entra ID?

a.explanation: Incorrect. PTA validates authentication against on-premises AD in real time — no password data is stored in the cloud.

b.explanation: Incorrect. ADFS is a federation solution where authentication is handled entirely by on-premises ADFS — no hash sync occurs.

c.explanation: Correct. Password Hash Sync synchronises a hash of the hash of the user's on-premises password to Entra ID. This enables cloud-based authentication without needing to reach the on-premises AD.

d.text: Entra ID Connect Cloud Sync
d.explanation: Incorrect. Entra Connect Cloud Sync is a lightweight sync agent, but it is the synchronisation tool — PHS is the authentication method. Cloud Sync can use PHS, but they are different concepts.

questions.question_text: An administrator wants to prevent anyone — including Global Administrators — from deleting a production virtual machine. What is the most direct way to achieve this?

a.text: Remove the Owner role from all administrators
a.explanation: Incorrect. RBAC roles can be reassigned. Removing roles does not prevent future role reassignment by other Owners.

b.text: Apply a CanNotDelete lock to the virtual machine
b.explanation: Correct. A CanNotDelete resource lock prevents deletion regardless of RBAC permissions. Even users with Owner role cannot delete the resource until the lock is removed.

c.text: Apply an Azure Policy with a Deny effect to block VM deletion
c.explanation: Incorrect. Azure Policy Deny effect blocks creation/updates — it cannot block delete operations in the same way a resource lock can.

d.text: Move the VM to a separate subscription
d.explanation: Incorrect. Moving the VM to another subscription does not protect it from deletion — you would still need a lock.

questions.question_text: A company wants external partners to access their Azure resources using the partners' existing corporate credentials. Which Entra ID feature should be used?

a.text: Create new Entra ID user accounts for each partner
a.explanation: Incorrect. Creating accounts requires managing passwords and licenses for external users — not the recommended approach.

b.explanation: Correct. B2B collaboration allows external partners to be invited as guest users who sign in with their own organisation's credentials (or Microsoft/Google accounts). No password management required on the host side.

c.explanation: Incorrect. B2C (Azure AD B2C) is for customer identity scenarios (consumer apps) — not for partner/business collaboration.

d.text: Federate the partner's AD with ADFS
d.explanation: Incorrect. ADFS federation is a complex on-premises solution. B2B guest invitations are simpler and more scalable for partner access.

questions.question_text: When an Azure subscription is transferred to a new billing owner, what happens to the existing RBAC role assignments?

a.text: All RBAC role assignments are automatically removed
a.explanation: Incorrect. Role assignments remain intact when billing ownership is transferred.

b.explanation: Correct. Transferring a subscription changes the billing owner but does not modify RBAC role assignments. Resources and access control remain as configured.

c.text: Only the Owner role assignments are removed
c.explanation: Incorrect. No role assignments are removed during a billing transfer.

d.text: The subscription must be re-created after transfer
d.explanation: Incorrect. Transfer moves the subscription with all resources and role assignments intact to the new billing account.

questions.question_text: Which Entra ID feature requires P2 licensing and provides just-in-time activation of privileged roles with approval workflows?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Conditional Access is available with P1 and controls access based on conditions — it does not provide JIT role activation.

b.explanation: Correct. PIM requires Entra ID P2 and provides just-in-time privileged access — users activate roles on demand for a limited duration, with optional approval workflows and audit trails.

c.explanation: Incorrect. Identity Protection (also P2) detects risky sign-ins and users — it does not manage role activation workflows.

d.text: Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR)
d.explanation: Incorrect. SSPR requires P1 (for on-premises writeback) and allows password resets — it has nothing to do with role activation.

questions.question_text: An Azure Policy assignment has the 'Audit' effect. What happens when a non-compliant resource is detected?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Azure Policy never automatically deletes resources — that is not a supported policy effect.

b.text: The resource operation is blocked and an error is returned
b.explanation: Incorrect. That describes the Deny effect, not Audit.

c.text: The resource is flagged as non-compliant in the policy compliance dashboard but no action is taken
c.explanation: Correct. The Audit effect evaluates the resource, marks it as non-compliant in the Policy Compliance dashboard, and generates an audit log entry — but allows the resource to exist and does not block any operations.

d.text: A remediation task is automatically triggered
d.explanation: Incorrect. Automatic remediation is triggered by the DeployIfNotExists and Modify effects, not Audit.

questions.question_text: A user account must be assigned a Microsoft 365 license. The license assignment fails with an error. What is the most likely missing prerequisite?

a.text: The user does not have MFA enabled
a.explanation: Incorrect. MFA is not a prerequisite for license assignment.

b.text: The user's Usage location property is not set
b.explanation: Correct. Azure requires a Usage location to be set on the user account before a license can be assigned. This is because license availability and features vary by country/region for compliance reasons.

c.text: The user's account is not synced from on-premises AD
c.explanation: Incorrect. Cloud-only users can also be assigned licenses — synced identity is not a requirement.

d.text: The user does not belong to any security group
d.explanation: Incorrect. Group membership is not required to assign a license directly to a user.

questions.question_text: Which scope is HIGHEST in the Azure management hierarchy and allows governance policies to be applied across ALL subscriptions in an organisation?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Subscriptions are below management groups in the hierarchy.

b.explanation: Incorrect. Resource groups are within subscriptions — they are the lowest scope in the hierarchy.

c.explanation: Correct. The Root management group is the top-level container. All subscriptions ultimately roll up to it. Policies and RBAC assigned here apply to every subscription and resource in the entire Entra tenant.

d.text: Entra ID Tenant
d.explanation: Incorrect. The tenant is the identity boundary, not an Azure management scope. The Root management group is the top of the Azure resource hierarchy.

questions.question_text: An administrator creates a CanNotDelete lock on a resource group. A developer tries to delete one of the resources within that resource group. What happens?

a.text: The deletion succeeds because the lock is on the resource group, not the individual resource
a.explanation: Incorrect. Resource locks on a resource group are inherited by all resources within the group. The delete operation will fail.

b.text: The deletion fails because resource group locks are inherited by child resources
b.explanation: Correct. Locks are inherited by all resources within the locked scope. A CanNotDelete lock on a resource group prevents deletion of any resource within that group.

c.text: The deletion succeeds if the developer has the Owner role
c.explanation: Incorrect. Resource locks override RBAC — even users with the Owner role cannot delete a resource protected by a CanNotDelete lock.

d.text: The developer is prompted to justify the deletion before it proceeds
d.explanation: Incorrect. Resource locks do not present a justification prompt — they simply block the operation with an error.

questions.question_text: Which Azure service provides recommendations for reducing Azure costs, including identifying idle virtual machines and suggesting reserved instances?

a.explanation: Incorrect. Azure Monitor provides observability (metrics, logs, alerts) — it does not provide cost optimisation recommendations.

b.explanation: Incorrect. Azure Policy enforces governance rules — it does not analyse cost patterns.

c.explanation: Correct. Azure Advisor analyses your Azure usage and provides recommendations across five categories: Cost, Security, Reliability, Operational Excellence, and Performance. The Cost category includes idle VM recommendations and reserved instance suggestions.

d.text: Azure Cost Analysis
d.explanation: Incorrect. Cost Analysis shows spending breakdowns and trends but does not proactively generate optimisation recommendations — that is Azure Advisor's role.
